item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I finished reading this the only thing I could think of was: FOR FINLAND!!!!!!!!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was definitely going for a theme like that with this. To be more specific…

Spoilery hints inside.
I like turning to the last page of the book first, too.

She absolutely meant it in an 'eternal punishment' sense. 3237 can't age or die by any practical means, as evidenced by him looking 20-40 despite being a good 70-90 years old. The Foundation could probably kill him, but that's more a technicality than anything.

3237's state of being isn't what the woman intended for him. He's the sole exception.

I like turning to the last page of the book first, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it a double meaning as in 'you deserve everything you've got coming to you' and 'you deserve to wander the earth for eternity', i.e. until everything has happened?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The final line is something I went out of my way to work in there, because it's the most direct hint to one of the main unstated mysteries about this SCP. Everything the woman said in the document I posted in this thread is worded very specifically to hint at her nature, her morals, and the intended goal of what she did. Hence why I had it in previous versions of the draft.

I've never been sure if any of my readers caught the meaning behind that line. The only people I'm absolutely sure know about it are the ones I've told. I'm looking forward to seeing if anyone can figure it out themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be honest, I actually have no idea how they'd go about confirming kills. But to clarify, the 174 -A's they specified as being confirmed kills were all actually recovered and buried after the war. They exhumed a few of the bodies from their graves to see if any of them were 3237, and kept going when they found the anomalous temperature and lack of decomposition.

The 63 they recovered from Kollaa were also killed by Häyhä, but may not count toward his confirmed kills. I don't know if you need to find the body for it to count. And in retrospect, 63 seems like a lot of bodies to miss for 60 years. Maybe me from three years ago should've made that number smaller…

Oh well. I used the number of bodies to pick the SCP number. Can't change it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the first two interviews, the third one didn't quite have the same level of relatability but works for what it is. Overall I quite enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty decent, actually. Good work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty great. The anomaly was just interesting enough to carry me into the interviews, where the meat is. The logical jump from 'sugar' to 'White Death' was fun to watch, and tying a skip in with the White Death is just a fantastic idea. (I didn't know he was alive into this century!) I do kind of feel like the final line isn't as punchy as it could be, but I could also chalk this up to this winter spirit (?) thinking in a very alien way. Still, great stuff, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little confused about how they were able to figure out that the -A instances were killed by Hayha. If they were part of his confirmed kills, then presumably someone would have been able to take them out of the woods or wherever they were. If not, then I'm not sure they can prove they were killed by him.

Otherwise, this was a pretty decent read. Good job, +1

item.title: Finally did it
item.content:encoded:
This thing has been sitting in my sandbox, almost finished, for the better part of three years. It eventually reached a point where most of my critique was starting to be about the same few things, which I fixed to the best of my ability. I had to get this thing out there, or I'd never be able to feel calm again.

Thanks to Petrograd in the forum and Modern_Erasmus in chat for recent critique, VAElynx and Kalinin in the forum and thekillerax in the chat for three years ago. I'd check how to mention your profiles but I'm too anxious to look it up right now.

I actually wrote up the exact thing the woman said, but most of my recent critique said it didn't add much and could be folded into the last two interview logs. I did that, but I still liked it enough that I'm compelled to post it here.

View Document 3237-A
Hide Document 3237-A

Häyhä wanted me to "let them know". I presume he meant all of you. I might as well tell you the same thing I told Häyhä.

I give people what they ask. I offer them what they deserve.

Simo Häyhä is the name of the man who shot you. He wanted to protect his country, so I helped him. You wanted to take what didn't belong to you, so he stopped you.

I offered you my Hell, and you accepted because you deserved it. It doesn't matter that you didn't know at the time. You've got plenty of time to think about it now.

I offered Häyhä my Heaven, and he refused the reward he deserved. Instead, he asked for oblivion.

I gave him what he asked. I think he felt it was what he deserved.

Enjoy your sleep Häyhä. Death will not bother you as I watch.

Nor will it bother all of you. But you don't get to sleep. You don't deserve oblivion.

You deserve less than nothing. You deserve everything.

Hide Document 3237-A
